title: How to Break Up with Your Phone
author: Catherine Price
subline: A 30-day digital detox for the chronically connected.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A simple smartphone outline (vertical rectangle with rounded corners), drawn in red 3px stroke. The screen is filled with a single hairline X (the break-up). -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1700)" fill="none">
    <!-- Phone outline -->
    <rect x="-200" y="-440" width="400" height="880" rx="40" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- Speaker (top) -->
    <line x1="-30" y1="-400" x2="30" y2="-400" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- Home button (bottom) -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="400" r="22" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- The big X across the screen — break up -->
    <line x1="-180" y1="-360" x2="180" y2="320" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="180" y1="-360" x2="-180" y2="320" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="2150" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.4em">IT'S NOT YOU, IT'S ME · 30 DAYS</text>
</svg>
rationale: A simple smartphone outline in red 3px stroke, with a giant red X across the screen. Speaker slit at top, home button at bottom, both in red. No graphite anywhere. The phone is the only object and the X is the break-up. Title 280px mixed case, three lines, weight 300. The composition is a single canceled device.
